sql_array: CREATE TABLE docs (id INT PRIMARY KEY, text VARCHAR(255), score DECIMAL(5,2));

INSERT INTO docs (id, text, score) VALUES (1, 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog', 0.85), (2, 'mocha with almond milk and spices', 0.75), (3, 'virtual database implementation and performance tuning', 0.95);
SELECT id, text, score FROM docs WHERE bif:contains(text, 'virtual database') AND contains(text, 'implementation') ORDER BY SCORE DESC;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM docs WHERE bif:contains(text, 'mocha')) AS mocha_count FROM docs GROUP BY id HAVING mocha_count > 0;
CREATE TABLE more_docs (doc_id INT, more_text TEXT, CHECK (length(more_text) > 0), FOREIGN KEY(doc_id) REFERENCES docs(id));
INSERT INTO more_docs (doc_id, more_text) VALUES (1, 'deep learning with python'), (2, 'data science and SQL queries'), (3, 'performance and optimization in databases');
SELECT d.id, d.text FROM docs d JOIN more_docs md ON d.id = md.doc_id WHERE bif:contains(md.more_text, 'data science') AND d.score > (SELECT AVG(score) FROM docs);

SELECT d.id, (CASE WHEN d.score IS NULL THEN 'No Score' ELSE 'Score Available' END) AS score_status FROM docs d WHERE bif:contains(d.text, 'performance') AND d.score IS NOT NULL ORDER BY d.score DESC;
SELECT MAX(d.score) AS max_score FROM docs d WHERE contains(d.text, 'fox') OR (d.text IS NULL);
SELECT text, score FROM docs WHERE bif:contains(text, 'lazy') AND score = (SELECT MAX(score) FROM docs);
